steps.title: Substitute to eliminate the repeated expression
steps.content:
Notice that $(2x - 7)$ appears twice in the equation. Let $u = 2x - 7$ to simplify. The equation becomes:
$$u^2 - 8u + 15 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic in u
steps.content:
Factor $u^2 - 8u + 15 = 0$ by finding two numbers that multiply to 15 and add to $-8$. Those numbers are $-3$ and $-5$:
$$(u - 3)(u - 5) = 0$$
Therefore, $u = 3$ or $u = 5$.

steps.title: Back-substitute to solve for x
steps.content:
Substitute $u = 2x - 7$ back into each solution:
  • If $u = 3$: $2x - 7 = 3$, so $2x = 10$, thus $x = 5$
  • If $u = 5$: $2x - 7 = 5$, so $2x = 12$, thus $x = 6$
The solutions are $x = 5$ and $x = 6$.
